changes_summary: Revised plan to address all 3 flags: (1) Items are now POSITIONED via sequential `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration` calls — not unpositioned. Timeline clips are sorted by `(trackIndex, action.start)` before insertion. (2) Shot naming uses existing `generateShotName()` from `useShotCreation` — no new naming convention. (3) Cross-surface selection clarified as independent (mutually exclusive by design); no selection merging attempted. Also removed Phase 4 floating action bar per user instruction ("skip, just do context menu for now"). Added gate for non-media timeline clips (hide menu items entirely). Simplified hook architecture — no separate `useCreateShotFromSelection` or `useGenerateVideoFromSelection` hooks; instead extend existing `useShotCreation` with a `generationIds` option and a new creation path.
questions: The `createShotWithGenerationPath` uses an RPC (`create_shot_with_image`) that atomically creates a shot + adds one image. For multi-gen, should we try to extend this RPC to accept multiple generation IDs, or is the two-step approach (create empty shot, then add images sequentially) acceptable? The two-step approach is simpler but non-atomic.

Should the context menu show a loading/spinner state while the shot is being created (sequential RPC calls may take 1-2 seconds for 5+ items), or should it close immediately and show progress elsewhere (e.g. skeleton in shot list)?

success_criteria.criterion: Right-clicking a selected gallery item shows a context menu with 'Create Shot' and 'Generate Video' options
'Create Shot' creates a new shot containing all currently selected items, all positioned (timeline_frame is not null), in selection order
'Generate Video' creates a new shot from selected items and opens VideoGenerationModal with that shot; modal shows positioned images and allows generation
Context menu appears correctly for both single-select and multi-select scenarios
Right-clicking an unselected item selects it first, then shows the context menu
Context menu dismisses on click-away, Escape key, and after completing an action
No regressions to existing gallery selection, lasso selection, drag-and-drop, or timeline context menu actions
New shots use existing auto-naming convention (Shot N) from generateShotName()
Timeline clip context menu includes Create Shot and Generate Video options only for clips with generation assets (hidden for text/effect/audio clips)
Timeline clips are positioned in the shot ordered by (track, start time), not by Set insertion order
Context menu uses existing design tokens (bg-popover, text-popover-foreground, shadow-md) and matches app visual style
Sequential shot creation handles 10+ items with visible loading feedback (spinner or skeleton)
New component files stay under ~300 lines each
Manual smoke tests pass for all 7 scenarios listed in Step 7

assumptions: VideoGenerationModal can be rendered outside the video travel tool's component tree — it only needs a Shot object, not ShotEditor context. Step 2 explicitly verifies this before proceeding.

Sequential insertion via `insertAutoPositionedShotGeneration` is fast enough for typical selections (< 20 items). Each call is a single RPC. If performance becomes an issue, a batch RPC can be added later.

Gallery selection order (`gallerySelectionMap` Map insertion order) is a reasonable proxy for user-intended image ordering. Lasso selects items top-left to bottom-right; click-selects preserve click order.
The mutual exclusion between gallery and timeline selection is intentional design — the context menu reads from whichever system currently has a selection, never both simultaneously.
The portal-based context menu pattern from ClipAction.tsx is the preferred approach (no new UI library or shadcn primitive needed).
